title: Component self-assembly for reciprocal urban mining
data_sources: Waste PCBs (WPCBs, Shanghai Xinjinqiao Environmental Protection)
Spent automobile catalysts (SACs, Ningda Precious Metals)

data_scope.experimental_scale: bench-top laboratory (~8 g batches per experiment)
data_scope.global_context_ewaste: 62 million metric tonnes/year (2022, UN Global E-waste Monitor)

core_conclusion: Urban mines (e-waste, spent catalysts, end-of-life vehicles) contain metals, oxides, polymers, and halogens that impose irreconcilable environmental-economic-efficiency trade-offs when recycled in isolation. A self-assembly strategy exploiting cross-waste componential interactions enables reciprocal recycling where waste constituents catalyse one another's transformation. Applied to WPCBs and spent automobile catalysts, this achieved >96% Cu-Pt-Pd-Rh co-recovery, >99% suppression of brominated pollutants, epoxy-resin chemical recycling, a 75.9% reduction in environmental footprints, and ~US$8,500/tonne increased product value.
why_it_matters: Breaks the persistent efficiency-environment-cost trade-off in e-waste recycling; addresses 62 Mt/yr of global e-waste with <25% proper recycling; provides mechanistic DFT transparency for rational process design; demonstrates extensibility to LIBs, glass-fibre polymers, LCD/PVC co-recycling, and other urban mines; underpins both economic value creation and pollution prevention simultaneously.
why_nature: Paradigm-shifting reconception of urban mining from isolated single-waste processing to a self-sufficient ecology; cross-disciplinary integration of chemical engineering, materials science, and sustainability science; directly addresses SDGs 7, 12, and 13; techno-economic and LCA analysis confirm industrial viability beyond proof-of-concept.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial co-location optimisation of complementary urban mine streams -> GeoAI facility-location model for reciprocal recycling plants
Componential interaction network -> GNN-based process design that parallels physics-informed graph learning for urban systems
Flash Joule heating hybrid integration -> multi-source data fusion framework for urban mining process-performance comparison
Urban mine composition shifts under EV transition -> spatiotemporal GeoAI mapping of supply-chain equity in green-energy cities
Informal recycling spatial distribution + health risk catchment areas -> 2SFCA/KDE methods bridging material sustainability and health geography

related.title: Urban mining by flash Joule heating
related.relation: complementary rapid-thermal precious-metal recovery from e-waste; potential hybrid process with self-assembly debromination pre-treatment

related.title: Sustainable battery recycling through spatial and technological alignment
related.relation: ML-integrated multi-scale framework for EOL battery recycling — directly extensible to self-assembly design for LIBs as urban mines

related.title: Impact of transport electrification on critical metal sustainability with a focus on the heavy-duty segment
related.relation: demand-side driver of SAC/LIB urban mine generation; contextualises the supply-chain resilience value of >96% PGM recovery

related.title: Advancing a slum–circular economy model for sustainability transition in cities of the Global South
related.relation: informal urban mining equity gap; integration challenge for self-assembly strategy in Global South recycling contexts

related.title: Worldwide scaling of waste generation in urban systems
related.relation: urban-scale waste generation patterns that determine spatial distribution and availability of urban mine feedstocks for reciprocal recycling

figures.caption: Urban mining status and self-assembly strategy of components — WPCB/photovoltaic composition, environmental-economic-efficiency imbalances, and the proposed bottom-up reciprocal recycling concept
Important componential interactions for reciprocal recycling under dry-thermal conditions — categorised by metal recovery, organic halogen detoxification, and polymer chemical recycling
Components self-assembly based on organic bromine-oxide interaction for brominated pollutants elimination — species evolution, CaO debromination mechanisms, and >99% Br mineralisation
Self-assembly strategy based on polymer-oxide interactions for polymer chemical recycling — epoxy resin conversion yields, BPA decomposition pathways, and DFT energy profiles on CaO(100)

Components self-assembly based on oxide-oxide and metal-metal interactions for metal co-recovery — HAADF-STEM imaging, Cu settling and Pt capture across 30 slag compositions, nanoparticle collision kinetics
Joint environmental-economic-efficiency benefits with self-assembly strategy and extension to LIBs, LCDs, glass-fibre polymers, and other urban mine combinations
